title: Cosmos Word Search — find the hidden cosmic words
summary: A real, self-contained word search puzzle in one HTML file with zero dependencies. A 10x10 grid of letters hides six cosmic words (COMET, NEBULA, ORBIT, QUASAR, PULSAR, GALAXY) running horizontally, vertically or diagonally in either direction. Click-drag (or tap a start cell then an end cell) across a straight run of letters to select it; if it spells a hidden word the run is highlighted with a coloured glow and the word is crossed off the list. A live timer and a FOUND counter track progress, you win when all six words are found, and R or the button reseeds a brand-new grid. It ships with a deterministic self-playing attract demo for capture: a seeded grid reveals each hidden word one by one with a sweeping highlight.
usage_notes: RUN: double-click index.html. CONTROLS: click-drag across letters to draw a selection, or tap the first letter then the last letter of a word; works horizontally, vertically and diagonally in both directions. Touch drag is supported via pointer events. GOAL: find all six hidden cosmic words; each correct word lights up with its own glow capsule and is struck through in the word list, the HUD shows FOUND x/6 and an elapsed TIME, and finding all six wins; press R or New Grid to reseed a fresh puzzle. KEY TECHNIQUES: the grid is generated by a deterministic mulberry32-seeded placer that tries word placements in 8 directions and back-fills random letters, with pickSeed scanning for a seed where all words fit; the AUTO attract demo is fully deterministic — autoState(ms) replays the placed-word list, sweeping each word's cells as a pure function of time so window.__seek(t) reproduces the exact grid, highlights, sweep, counter and timer from t alone. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: REVEAL_MS sweep speed per word, GAP_MS pause between words, HOLD_MS finished hold, T0OFF (frame-0 offset), GRID size, WORDS list, seed start in pickSeed.
